title: Riot Platforms Full Year 2023 Earnings: EPS Beats Expectations, Revenues Lag
text: Riot Platforms reported mixed results for fiscal year 2023. The company generated $280.7 million in revenue, representing 8.3% growth compared to the prior year, but this performance fell short of analyst expectations by 2.7%. The company demonstrated significant profitability improvement with its net loss narrowing dramatically to $49.5 million, a 90% reduction from the previous year's loss. This translated to a loss per share of $0.28, substantially better than the prior year's $3.65 loss per share. Notably, Riot's earnings per share exceeded analyst estimates by 71%, even as revenues underperformed projections. Bitcoin Mining revenue reached $189.0 million compared to $156.9 million in 2022. Data Center Hosting revenue was $27.3 million compared to $36.9 million in 2022. Engineering revenue was $64.3 million compared to $65.3 million in 2022. The company produced 6,626 Bitcoin in 2023, compared to 5,554 in 2022, a 19% increase. The company reported non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA of $214.0 million in 2023 which included a $184.7 million gain on Bitcoin held on the balance sheet. Riot is positioned for stronger growth ahead, with revenue projected to expand at 49% annually over the next two years—significantly outpacing the broader software industry's anticipated 12% growth rate.
